audit_items.proof_trace: jit.c contains exactly two dynamic allocations: `kvmalloc_array` in `nfp_bpf_prog_trim` (L4457) and `kmemdup` in `nfp_bpf_relo_for_vnic` (L4540). The `kvmalloc_array` result either fails (immediate return, no leak) or is assigned to `nfp_prog->prog` after freeing the old buffer; it is ultimately freed by `nfp_bpf_destroy` in offload.c via `kvfree`. The `kmemdup` result is freed via `err_free_prog` on all error paths and returned to the caller (who correctly calls `kfree(img)`) on success — no execution path leaks memory.
